mapping.description: Array of touchpoint-to-screen routing entries that determines which screen configuration to display for a given touchpoint. The resolver (SubscriptionOfferServiceNewImpl) finds the first entry whose touchpoint matches the runtime value or falls back to an entry with touchpoint 'default', then uses its screen value to look up the corresponding screen in the screens array. Changing the order or contents controls which alert screen appears for each entry-point in the app.

subscription_status.description: Not consumed by the resolver used for this feature (SubscriptionOfferServiceNewImpl only matches by touchpoint) — changing it has no visible effect. Present in the data model for use by StatusBasedTouchPointResolver in other feature paths.

screen.description: Screen identifier that links this mapping entry to a screen configuration in the screens array. Must exactly match the screen_name value of the target screen. Accepted values: any string matching a screen_name in the screens array. Changing this routes the associated touchpoint to a different screen configuration, altering the entire alert UI shown to the user.

subscription_statuses.description: Not consumed by the resolver used for this feature (SubscriptionOfferServiceNewImpl only matches by touchpoint) — changing it has no visible effect. Present in the data model for use by StatusBasedTouchPointResolver in other feature paths.

touchpoint.description: Touchpoint identifier matched against the runtime touchpoint parameter passed from the payment or subscription flow. If no exact match is found, the resolver falls back to the entry with value 'default'. Accepted values: any touchpoint string (e.g. from SubscriptionOfferTooltipTouchPointNames) or 'default'. Changing this controls which app entry-point maps to the associated screen.

enabled.description: Not consumed by the resolver used for this feature (SubscriptionOfferServiceNewImpl does not check this flag) — changing it has no visible effect. The resolver matches touchpoints without filtering by enabled status.

screens.description: Array of screen configurations defining the full visual layout and behavior of alert screens. Each entry is identified by screen_name and referenced from the mapping array. The SubscriptionAlertFragment renders the matched screen using its background, banner, text, button, and checklist fields.

thankYouType.description: Determines the screen flow type used in ShopSubscribeActivity to decide between trial-end and regular thank-you flows. For the trial_end_alert_screen_sf settings key, the code always overrides this to TRIAL_END via copy() in SubscriptionOnBoardingRepoImpl.getTrialEndAlertScreenRx(). Accepted values: 'TRIAL_END', 'REGULAR'. Changing this in JSON has no visible effect because the code overrides it at runtime.

close_action.description: Mapped to SubscriptionAlert.onOutSideTouchAction via subscriptionAlertMapper but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

skip_button.description: Mapped to SubscriptionAlert.skipButton entity via skipButtonMapper but not rendered in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it or any of its child properties has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

bg_color.description: Mapped to SkipButton.backgroundColor but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

show_from_page.description: Mapped to SkipButton.showFromPage (page index after which the skip button appears) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

close_button.description: Mapped to SkipButton.button (SubscriptionCloseButton entity) via closeButtonMapper but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it or any of its child properties has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

show_delay.description: Mapped to SubscriptionCloseButton.showDelay (delay in milliseconds before the close button appears) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

timer_timout.description: Mapped to SubscriptionCloseButton.timerTimeout (timeout in milliseconds for auto-dismiss timer) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

ic_url.description: Mapped to SubscriptionCloseButton.icUrl (URL of the close button icon) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

show.description: Mapped to SubscriptionCloseButton.show (visibility flag) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

alignment.description: Mapped to SubscriptionCloseButton.alignment via CloseButtonAlignmentModel but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment. The code maps 'left' to CloseButtonAlignment.LEFT and 'right' to CloseButtonAlignment.RIGHT. Changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

title.description: Mapped to SubscriptionCloseButton.title (TextConfig with text and color for the close button label) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

color.description: Mapped to TextConfig.color for the close button label but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

text.description: Mapped to TextConfig.text for the close button label but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

keep_after_showing.description: Mapped to SkipButton.keepAfterShowing (whether the skip button remains visible after first appearance) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

action.description: Mapped to SkipButton.action (navigation action triggered on skip tap) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

text.description: Mapped to SkipButton.text (TextConfig with text and color for the skip button label) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

color.description: Mapped to TextConfig.color for the skip button label but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

text.description: Mapped to TextConfig.text for the skip button label but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

type.description: Mapped to SkipButton.type via CloseButtonTypeModel (DEFAULT renders as icon button, TEXT renders as text button) but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

banner.description: Banner media configuration displayed at the top of the alert screen. Rendered in SubscriptionAlertFragment via initSubscriptionBannerView(). If the banner object is null, no media is shown. When present, the type determines the rendering component (LottieAnimationView for Lottie, MediaView for all others).

type.description: Determines the media rendering component. The code checks for BannerType.LOTTIE to render via LottieAnimationView (requires network connectivity); all other values (IMAGE, VIDEO, GIF, REVIEWS) render via MediaView using the type as a lowercase string in MediaViewData. Accepted values via @SerializedName: 'image', 'video', 'gif', 'lottie', 'reviews'. Changing this switches how the banner URL is loaded and displayed.

url.description: URL of the banner media resource (image, video, GIF, or Lottie JSON). If blank, the banner is skipped entirely (initSubscriptionBannerView returns early). For LOTTIE type, loaded via setAnimationFromUrl(); for others, loaded via MediaView. Changing this swaps the visual media displayed at the top of the alert screen.

checklist.description: Checklist configuration rendered as a vertical RecyclerView of checkmark items via CheckMarkAdapter in SubscriptionAlertFragment.fillCheckMark(). When present, the analytics screen ID is set to 'fullscreen' and the banner media view is resized to 115×115px. When absent, the checklist RecyclerView remains hidden and the banner retains its default size.

values.description: Array of checklist items, each rendered as a row with a checkmark icon and text in the RetentionCheckMarkViewHolder. Each item's text and color are displayed in the RecyclerView. Adding, removing, or reordering items changes the visible checklist rows on the alert screen.

color.description: Hex color string applied to this checklist item's text via setTextColor() in RetentionCheckMarkViewHolder. Accepted values: hex color strings (e.g. '#FFFFFF'). Changing this alters the text color of the individual checklist row.

text.description: Text content displayed next to the checkmark icon for this checklist item. Rendered in checkMarkTxtView of RetentionCheckMarkViewHolder. Changing this updates the visible label of the checklist row.

checkbox_color.description: Hex color string applied to all checkmark icons via setColorFilter() in RetentionCheckMarkViewHolder. If empty, falls back to the default '#E5202C' (red). Accepted values: hex color strings. Changing this recolors all checkmark icons in the checklist.

button.description: Primary CTA button configuration rendered at the bottom of the alert screen via SubscriptionButtonView.initButton(). The button's action field determines screen behavior: 'onboarding' triggers a transition animation to the onboarding cards flow, while any other value opens a navigation hook via openHook(). When null, no button is shown.

background_color.description: Hex color string for the button background, used to create a GradientDrawable in SubscriptionButtonView. Also carried through the fragment transition animation via getBackgroundColor() when transitioning to onboarding cards. Accepted values: hex color strings (e.g. '#6236FF'). Changing this alters the button's fill color and the transition animation background.

subtitle.description: Secondary text displayed below the title on the button, set via setTextIntoViewWithCheck() in SubscriptionButtonView. If empty or null, the subtitle view is hidden. Accepted values: any string, supports spannable price formatting with special characters. Changing this updates or hides the secondary label on the button.

icon.description: URI for a badge or discount indicator displayed on the button via the discountTextView area in SubscriptionButtonView. Accepted values: URL strings or empty. Changing this updates or hides the icon/badge element on the button.

action.description: Navigation action string determining button click behavior. If equal to 'onboarding', the screen is treated as a thank-you screen and clicking triggers a transition animation to the onboarding cards fragment. Otherwise, the value is parsed as a URI and passed to openHook() for deep-link navigation. Accepted values: 'onboarding' or any valid hook URI string. Changing this alters what happens when the user taps the button.

had_title.description: Alternate title text displayed on the button when the user previously had a subscription. Mapped to SimpleButton.hadContent.title via simpleButtonHadTextToParagraph() and used by SubscriptionButtonView.initButton() to swap button text for returning subscribers. Accepted values: any string, supports spannable price formatting. Changing this updates the button title seen by returning subscribers.

style.description: Determines the button rendering style. FILL creates a solid filled background using GradientDrawable; STROKE creates an outlined button with a border. Also affects text color logic in SubscriptionButtonView. Accepted values via @SerializedName: 'fill', 'stroke'. Changing this toggles between a filled and outlined button appearance.

action_gold_user.description: Alternate action string used for gold/subscribed users, mapped to SimpleButton.actionGoldUser. Available in the entity for SubscriptionButtonView to route subscribed users differently. Accepted values: any valid hook URI string or empty. Changing this alters the navigation destination when a subscribed user taps the button.

text_color.description: Hex color string for the button title and subtitle text, applied via setTextColor() in SubscriptionButtonView. The actual color may be adjusted based on button style (FILL vs STROKE) and free-trial state. Accepted values: hex color strings (e.g. '#FFFFFF'). Changing this alters the button label color.

title.description: Primary text displayed on the button, set via setTitle() in SubscriptionButtonView. Supports spannable price formatting with special characters parsed by setSubscriptionPriceWithSpannable(). Accepted values: any string. Changing this updates the main label on the CTA button.

had_sub_title.description: Alternate subtitle text displayed on the button when the user previously had a subscription. Mapped to SimpleButton.hadContent.subtitle via simpleButtonHadTextToParagraph() and used by SubscriptionButtonView.initButton(). Accepted values: any string, supports spannable price formatting. Changing this updates the button subtitle seen by returning subscribers.

background_colors.description: Array of hex color strings used to create a top-to-bottom GradientDrawable on the alert screen's parent layout via setGradientBackgroundDrawable(). Takes priority over background_color when present. Accepted values: list of hex color strings (e.g. ['#1A1A2E', '#16213E']). Changing this alters the gradient background of the entire alert screen.

background_color.description: Hex color string for a solid background on the alert screen's parent layout, applied via Color.parseColor() and setBackgroundColor(). Used as fallback only when background_colors is null. Accepted values: hex color strings (e.g. '#1A1A2E'). Changing this alters the solid background color of the alert screen when no gradient is defined.

screen_name.description: Unique identifier for this screen configuration, used to link it to mapping entries. The resolver matches this against the screen value from the mapping array. Accepted values: any unique string. Changing this requires updating the corresponding mapping entry's screen field to maintain the link; otherwise the screen will not be resolved.

skip_button_action.description: Mapped to SubscriptionAlert.skipButtonAction via subscriptionAlertMapper but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

full_screen_lottie.description: URL of a Lottie animation played as a full-screen overlay via lottieAnimationView.setAnimationFromUrl() in SubscriptionAlertFragment. Only loaded when the device has network connectivity (checked via networkStatusService.isConnected()). Accepted values: URL string pointing to a Lottie JSON file. Changing this swaps the overlay animation; removing it hides the overlay entirely.

open_popup_automatically.description: Mapped to SubscriptionAlert.openPopupAutomatically (defaults to false via orFalse()) via subscriptionAlertMapper but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

indicator_color.description: Mapped to SubscriptionAlert.indicatorColor via subscriptionAlertMapper but not consumed in SubscriptionAlertFragment — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

text.description: Text section configuration containing a title and subtitle displayed in the ParagraphView of the alert screen. Rendered via paragraphView.initDescriptionView() in SubscriptionAlertFragment with title styled as Typography.T7/FontWeights.MEDIUM and subtitle styled with TEXT_SIZE_16 and max 3 lines. When null, the paragraph area remains empty.

subtitle.description: Subtitle text configuration rendered below the title in ParagraphView. Displayed with 16sp text size, maximum 3 lines, and applied color. Supports spannable price formatting with special characters via setSubscriptionPriceWithSpannable(). When null, the subtitle area remains empty.

color.description: Hex color string applied to the subtitle text via setTextColor() in ParagraphView.setTextViewParams(). Accepted values: hex color strings (e.g. '#FFFFFF'). Changing this alters the subtitle text color on the alert screen.

text.description: Subtitle text content displayed in the ParagraphView below the title. Supports spannable price formatting with special delimiter characters. Accepted values: any string. Changing this updates the descriptive subtitle text on the alert screen.

vertical_alignment.description: Vertical positioning of the text section within the ParagraphView layout, mapped via ParagraphTextAlignmentModel to ParagraphTextAlignment. Used by ParagraphView's ConstraintSet for vertical layout management. Accepted values via @SerializedName: 'left', 'center', 'right', 'bottom'. Changing this shifts the vertical position of the title and subtitle within their container.

id.description: Identifier for the text section, mapped to Paragraph.id. ParagraphView generates its own view IDs dynamically via View.generateViewId() and does not consume this field for rendering — changing it has no visible effect in the current trial_end_alert feature.

title.description: Title text configuration rendered as the heading in ParagraphView. Styled with Typography.T7 (cascade style) and FontWeights.MEDIUM. Supports spannable price formatting with special delimiter characters via setSubscriptionPriceWithSpannable(). When null, the title area remains empty.

color.description: Hex color string applied to the title text via setTextColor() in ParagraphView.setTextViewParams(). Accepted values: hex color strings (e.g. '#FFFFFF'). Changing this alters the title heading color on the alert screen.

text.description: Title text content displayed as the heading in ParagraphView. Supports spannable price formatting with special delimiter characters. Accepted values: any string. Changing this updates the heading text on the alert screen.

alignment.description: Horizontal text alignment for both title and subtitle in ParagraphView, applied via Gravity. Maps 'left' to Gravity.START and 'center' to Gravity.CENTER via ParagraphTextAlignmentModel. Accepted values via @SerializedName: 'left', 'center', 'right', 'bottom'. Changing this shifts the horizontal alignment of all text in the paragraph area.

tree.role: Entry point for trial end alert

children.role: Host activity for alert and onboarding fragments
Maps onboarding API models to entities
Maps special thank you API models to entities
Special thank you API response model
Subscription API service for settings by tag
Provides user subscription info for special thank you
